subject: English
chapter: Poems – How to Tell Wild Animals, The Ball Poem

activities_projects.title: Listing Animals
activities_projects.objective: Identify the animals mentioned in the poem.
steps: Read 'How to Tell Wild Animals' carefully
List all animals mentioned in the poem
Write one simple fact about each animal as described
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students recall key animals and basic characteristics.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correctness of animal list and facts

activities_projects.title: Simple Humor Identification
activities_projects.objective: Spot humorous lines in 'How to Tell Wild Animals'.
steps: Read the poem aloud
Underline lines that sound funny or surprising
Write why they are humorous in one or two sentences

activities_projects.title: Understanding Loss
activities_projects.objective: Learn about feelings of loss in 'The Ball Poem'.
steps: Read 'The Ball Poem'
Write how the boy feels after losing his ball
Discuss briefly with a partner why loss is important in life
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students relate poem theme to simple emotions.
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and relevance of written feelings

activities_projects.title: Finding Rhymes
activities_projects.objective: Identify simple rhymes in 'How to Tell Wild Animals'.
steps: Read the poem carefully
List 5 pairs of rhyming words
Say how rhymes make the poem fun to read
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct rhyme pairs and simple explanation

activities_projects.title: Drawing a Scene
activities_projects.objective: Visualize a part of the poem.
steps: Pick a simple scene from either poem
Draw it showing animals or the boy and ball
Label one or two key elements
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect words with visual imagination.

activities_projects.title: Acting Emotions
steps: Take turns acting as the boy losing the ball
Express how he might feel
Discuss briefly why acting helps understand feelings
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students express simple emotions and empathy.

activities_projects.title: Learning New Words
activities_projects.objective: Understand new words from the poems.
steps: Pick 3 new words from each poem
Write the meanings in simple words
Make a short sentence for each word
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students expand vocabulary with basic usage.

activities_projects.title: Comparing Poems Simply
activities_projects.objective: Notice differences between the two poems.
steps: Write one main idea for each poem
Say which poem is funny and which is serious
Discuss how tone changes your feelings

activities_projects.title: Question-Answer Practice
steps: Teacher asks simple questions like 'Which animal hugs very hard?'
Write answers in your notebook
Check answers with a partner
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students recall simple facts from the poems.

activities_projects.title: Retelling the Poem
steps: Read one poem carefully
Retell it in 3–5 sentences in your own words
Share with a partner or teacher
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students practice basic comprehension and summarization.
